purpose: Decompose Phase 5 (Renderer + Input) into 5.A.1 / 5.A.2 / 5.B sub-phases mirroring the established X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B pattern from Phases 3 and 4. Also record post-approval verification of Phase 4.B's cross-OS matrix going green for commit 484060e.
trigger: Phase 4.B was approved at HEAD of commit 484060e on 2026-05-04T14:46Z based on the existing deploy workflow being green for the prior phase-4.A.2 commit (run 25325562474) plus the new cross-os-atlas-equality matrix workflow being added to .github/workflows/deploy.yml. The matrix's first execution happened on the push of 484060e itself (CI run 25325869817), as anticipated by the Phase 4.B approval's 'chicken-and-egg' note. That run is now complete: status=completed, conclusion=success, all six jobs (build & test, cross-os atlas equality on ubuntu/macos/windows, the aggregator's pairwise SHA-256 equality assertion, and deploy-pages) green. The deterministic-encoder discipline pinned in addendum B4 + decision 4 (filter type 0 universally, fflate level 1 sync deflate, Uint8Array only, no native code, no Node-vs-browser branch) holds empirically across all three OSes — the per-OS hashes were byte-identical and the aggregator step printed all three for the audit record. Phase 4 is now end-to-end verified and Phase 5 is unblocked.

jobs.build_and_test: completed/success (1m 56s; lint, typecheck, unit tests + coverage, build, bundle size budget, cross-runtime e2e on chromium/firefox/webkit, deploy artifact upload)
jobs.cross_os_atlas_equality_ubuntu_latest: completed/success (17s; npm ci + npm run gen-atlas + git diff --exit-code -- assets/ + upload assets/atlas.png as atlas-png-ubuntu-latest)
jobs.cross_os_atlas_equality_windows_latest: completed/success (44s; same steps; Windows is the slowest shard due to runner provisioning)
jobs.cross_os_atlas_equality_aggregate: completed/success (5s; downloads all three atlas-png-* artifacts, computes sha256 via sha256sum, asserts pairwise byte equality with explicit per-OS hash logging — all three matched ATLAS_DIGEST = d1b4a8b73d3e2c1b7cd70c26fe15a08faae5d91351d9e2e9a542ce71727b8d1a)
jobs.deploy_to_github_pages: completed/success (9s; gated on both build AND cross-os-atlas-equality-aggregate per the Phase 4.B workflow extension)

phase_4_b_post_approval_verification.verdict: Phase 4 is end-to-end verified. The deterministic-encoder discipline holds across ubuntu-latest / macos-latest / windows-latest. No phase-update fix-up required.

phase_5_split_decision.rationale: Phase 5 (Renderer + Input) is comparable in surface area to Phase 4.A.2: it crosses three new layers (src/render, src/input, src/ui), wires the existing src/atlas/loader.ts into the runtime startup path for the first time, transforms the diagnostic page into a playable game while preserving the diagnostic determinism surface, and requires live-deploy verification on chromium/firefox/webkit (the X.B chicken-and-egg pattern). The X.A.1/X.A.2/X.B decomposition that Phases 3 and 4 used is the right fit: a small drift-detection sweep + ARCHITECTURE.md update before the implementation lands, the implementation in a single sandbox-verifiable commit, then live-deploy verification post-push. Phase 5 is NOT a planning-gate phase per docs/PHASES.md:7 (planning gates are 1.A, 2, 3, 4, 8 only), so the decomposition is recorded in this phase-update artifact rather than a decision memo.

phase_5_A_1.name: drift-detection sweep
phase_5_A_1.scope: Address Phase 3.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards where they touch files Phase 5.A.2 will modify (specifically applyFloorEntry's redundant param if Phase 5.A.2 calls it, the tick unknown-action defensive type-check if Phase 5.A.2 wires input→tick); address Phase 4.A.2 cosmetic carry-forwards N4 (loader JSON.parse cast→eslint-disable comment with justification at the data-ingestion boundary), N5 (EMPTY_SHA256 / PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION dual-source byte-equality assertion test between scripts/vite-plugin-atlas-binary-hash.mjs and src/build-info.ts), N6 (RecipeContext type colocated in src/atlas/recipes/floor.ts → relocate to src/atlas/recipes/types.ts so other recipes don't transitively import floor), N7 (explicit fflate-version assertion in the atlas-encoder-cross-runtime self-test); update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md with Phase 5 frozen contracts (the new src/render/, src/input/, src/ui/ layer-table entries with their import-allowed and import-forbidden lists; the renderer's read-only contract on sim state; the input descriptor's relationship to the Phase 1 Action schema; the HUD's read-only contract on RunState; the atlas-loader call site at run start; the lint rule inventory additions for the new layers). NO net-new src/render/**, src/input/**, or src/ui/** code lands in 5.A.1.
phase_5_A_1.expected_diff_size: small — primarily docs/ARCHITECTURE.md additions + a few cosmetic source patches + a cross-source byte-equality assertion test.

phase_5_A_2.name: sandbox-verifiable implementation
phase_5_A_2.scope: src/render/canvas.ts (tile renderer using the atlas blits via canvas drawImage; reads sim state read-only; frame loop driven by sim ticks rather than rAF for determinism); src/input/keyboard.ts (keypress event handler producing Action descriptors per the Phase 1 schema, no Math.random or floats); src/ui/hud.ts (HP / eddies / floor indicator / fingerprint widget; reads RunState.player + RunState.outcome + RunState.fingerprintInputs read-only); the atlas-loader integration in src/main.ts's startup path (calls loadAtlas() from src/atlas/loader.ts, refusing on PLACEHOLDER_RULESET_VERSION or hash mismatch with the addendum-N7-pinned messages); the diagnostic→playable transition in src/main.ts (the existing self-test banner + build-info + floor-preview + scripted-playthrough + atlas-preview sections continue to render so the Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B Playwright assertions keep passing alongside the new playable-game UI); the layer-import lint rules (src/render/** cannot import src/core/streams.ts or src/sim/combat.ts or any sim write paths; src/input/** cannot import sim write paths; src/ui/** cannot write to sim state); the architectural test asserting render's read-only contract (a runtime test that calls the renderer with a sealed sim state and confirms no mutations happened).
phase_5_A_2.expected_diff_size: large — comparable to Phase 4.A.2's surface area (renderer + input + HUD + integration + lint rules + architectural tests + main.ts transition; new test files for each new module).

phase_5_B.name: live-deploy + cross-runtime verification
phase_5_B.scope: Verify the live GitHub Pages URL serves the playable-game UI alongside the diagnostic sections. Verify cross-runtime Playwright on chromium/firefox/webkit: a new e2e test loads the page, simulates five movement keypresses (arrow keys or WASD per the keyboard.ts implementation), asserts the HUD updates (HP / floor / fingerprint visible; sim state advances per the scripted action sequence on a fixed seed). Verify the Phase 4.B cross-os-atlas-equality matrix continues to pass (the renderer changes are JS-only and don't regenerate the atlas, so ATLAS_DIGEST + the four preset-seed expectedHash values remain unchanged; drift would be a regression and a Phase 5.B blocker). Phase 5 is complete when 5.0 (no-op for non-planning-gate phase), 5.A.1, 5.A.2, and 5.B are all approved.
phase_5_B.expected_diff_size: minimal — observational verification + maybe one new Playwright test in tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts. The X.B verification commit's CI assertions only fire on the next push of the X.B commit itself per the established Phase 1.B/2.B/3.B/4.B chicken-and-egg pattern.

phase_5_split_decision.docs_changes: docs/PHASES.md updated with the Phase 5 split callout block (mirroring the Phase 3 lines 131-148 and Phase 4 lines 191-224 callouts) and the partitioned acceptance criteria for 5.A.1 / 5.A.2 / 5.B. No other docs changes required for this phase-update.

next_phase: Phase 5.A.1 — drift-detection sweep per the acceptance criteria recorded in docs/PHASES.md. Lead agent: code-reviewer or engine-builder for the cosmetic carry-forwards; the docs/ARCHITECTURE.md update can be drafted directly. Reviewer: code-reviewer for approval of the drift-sweep deliverables. The next iteration of the agent loop should pick up phase-5.A.1 from the docs/PHASES.md acceptance criteria.
